## Top Tips for Buying Cheap Cars in Wolverhampton

Even with the assurance of an MOT, it's always wise to do your due diligence. Here are a few helpful tips to ensure you make the best purchase:

### 1. Always Check the MOT History

While a valid MOT is great, checking its past history online can reveal recurring advisories or issues. This free service on GOV.UK gives you a fuller picture of the car’s maintenance over time.

### 2. View the Car in Daylight

Arrange to see the car during the day. Natural light helps you spot any cosmetic damage, rust, or inconsistencies in paintwork that might be hidden under artificial lighting or in the dark.

### 3. Take a Thorough Test Drive

Don't just drive around the block! Take the car on a variety of roads – slow urban streets, faster main roads, and even a short stretch of motorway if possible. Listen for unusual noises, check the brakes, steering, and all gears. Pay attention to how the car handles and feels.

### 4. Review the Service History

A comprehensive service history is a strong indicator that the previous owner took good care of the car. Look for regular servicing records, especially for older or higher mileage vehicles.
